title: Descending Platform
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2839q","text":"Behavioral test to assess motor function.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"981q2","text":"The test is using a custom made 45-degree grid path with a width of 5cm and a length of 45cm. The 15cm one side is oriented with a 45-degree angle. The grid is formed by metal mesh with a 0.5cm space between.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1d7dr","text":"Horizontal grid: custom made 45-degree grid path with a width of 5cm and a length of 45cm. 15cm of one side is oriented with a 45-degree angle. The grid is formed by metal mesh with a 0.5cm space between.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the custom-made, metal mesh grid to have an angle of 45 degrees with the floor.
At the end of the platform, place the animal cage to provide a safe target.
Place a camera above the grid to record the entire procedure. Here we use the Logitech C920 webcam and windows camera software.
Using the video, record the time for each mouse to go from:1- hindlimb cross the starting spot line to 2- hindlimb touch the cage on the bottom.
Using 5 randomly selected frames of the period the mice descend the platform, measure the distance between the hindlimb of the mice, average the result to get usable values.
Orient the grid to have the starting spot horizontal.
Take the mouse out of its cage using a paper tube to avoid stress and gently place the mouse on the starting spot.
Allow the mouse to explore the starting spot for 2-3 minutes.
Gently push the mice towards the tilted part of the grid path.
As soon as the mouse's hindlimbs are crossing the separation between the starting spot andthe tilted spot, leave the mouse alone.
As soon as the mouse reaches the cage, stop the camera and place the cage back into the ventilated rack.
Clean the platform with 50% ethanol solution to remove stress-related smells.
